# LinkedIn Post — NVIDIA Launches NemoClaw Enterprise AI Agent Platform at GTC 2026, Bets Big on Inference Revenue
# Week: Week of 2026-03-16
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-03-23T00:02:38.551Z

---

Enterprise AI agents just got their infrastructure layer.

At GTC 2026, Jensen Huang unveiled NemoClaw — NVIDIA's enterprise-grade AI agent platform built on top of OpenClaw, the open-source framework for building and running AI agents on a company's own hardware. Alongside this, NVIDIA positioned inference as its next major revenue frontier. IBM simultaneously announced an expanded collaboration with NVIDIA focused on sovereign AI infrastructure with regional data residency.

This is a deliberate play to own the enterprise agent stack. NemoClaw addresses the two objections that have stalled agent adoption in the C-suite: governance and infrastructure control. By enabling on-premises agent deployment through open-source foundations, NVIDIA is making it possible for enterprises to run autonomous AI workflows without sending data off-site.

The inference bet is equally significant. As enterprises move from training models to deploying agents that execute tasks in real time, inference workloads will dwarf training in compute demand. NVIDIA is building the hardware, the software platform, and the partnership ecosystem — including IBM's sovereign compliance layer — to capture that entire value chain.

For CTOs evaluating agentic AI, this changes the build-versus-buy calculus. The platform layer is arriving faster than most enterprise roadmaps anticipated.

GYanTech AI helps organizations design and deploy agentic AI architectures that align with their governance, infrastructure, and business requirements -> www.gyantechai.com

What would your first production-grade AI agent deployment look like if infrastructure and governance were no longer the blockers?

## Fact-check notes
- NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang announced NemoClaw, an enterprise-grade AI agent platform built on the open-source OpenClaw framework, during the GTC 2026 keynote (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NemoClaw enables enterprises to build and run AI agents locally on their own hardware (Source: TechCrunch, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA highlighted AI inference as a major chip revenue opportunity at GTC 2026 (Source: Reuters, March 16, 2026)
- NVIDIA GTC 2026 revealed the Vera Rubin architecture along with updated AI software platforms (Source: SaasNik / NVIDIA GTC, March 16, 2026)
- IBM announced expanded collaboration with NVIDIA at GTC 2026 to advance enterprise AI, including sovereign AI infrastructure with regional data residency (Source: IBM Newsroom, March 16, 2026)
